hero_sub: Flobro puts a tiny, frameless browser window above everything on your screen. Watch a video, follow a match, keep an eye on a dashboard, all while you work, game or study.
dl_note: Free and open source. MIT license. ~5 MB download.
demo_hint: ↑ hover the window, that's the whole interface
feat_title: No interface. Until you want one.
feat_sub: Flobro windows are pure content: no tabs, no address bar, no chrome. Move your mouse to the top edge and a slim toolbar slides in. Move away and it disappears.
f1_p: Your floating window stays above every app. Toggle it per window with the pin whenever you need normal stacking.
f2_p: Frameless and silent. The hover toolbar gives you zoom, pin, minimize, settings and close, then gets out of the way.
f3_p: Built with Tauri and Rust on the OS webview. A ~5 MB download that is easy on your memory and battery.
f4_p: One click snaps the window to perfect video proportions. Resize freely from any edge at any time.
f5_p: Scale pages from 25% to 500% so a small floating window still shows exactly what you care about.
f6_p: MIT licensed. Read the code, file issues, send pull requests.
s1_p: Open Flobro and drop in any URL: a video, stream, dashboard, docs page, anything on the web.
s2_p: The page opens in a clean frameless window that stays on top of your other apps.
s3_p: Move it, resize it, forget it. Hover the top edge whenever you need the controls.
ext_sub: The Flobro extension pops any tab, link or video out into a minimal window, right from the toolbar or the right-click menu. And with the desktop app installed, one click forwards the page to a true always-on-top window.
os_title: Open source, kept alive by you.
os_sub: Flobro has been free and open source since 2016. If it earns a floating spot on your screen, consider supporting development.
pp_lede: Flobro is built in the European Union, has no commercial party behind it, and does not make money from your data. Here is exactly what happens and why.
pp_cookies_p: This website sets no cookies, so there is no banner to click away. It does count visits with PostHog, a privacy-friendly analytics tool on EU servers: how many people visit, from which country (without storing your IP address), and which buttons get clicked, like the download and donate buttons, and whether the demo window on the homepage gets played with. Your language choice is stored in your own browser (localStorage) and never leaves it.
pp_app_p1: To learn what to improve, Flobro counts a few anonymous events using PostHog, a privacy-friendly analytics tool, on servers in the EU. What gets counted:
pp_app_li2: that a page was floated, together with only its hostname, like youtube.com
pp_app_li3: your system language, so we know which translations to add next
pp_app_p2: What never gets sent: full web addresses, page titles, what you watch or read, your IP address, or anything that identifies you. Each install gets a random id that says nothing about who you are.
pp_optout_p: In the app: Settings, then switch off "Share anonymous usage stats". In the extension: right-click the Flobro icon, choose Options, and uncheck it there. Everything keeps working exactly the same.
pp_why_p: Flobro is a free, open source side project. Knowing that most people float video sites, or that many users run a Dutch system, decides what gets built next. That is the whole story: usage numbers in, better app out. Nothing is sold, shared or combined with other data.
pp_contact_p: Open an issue on the source repository, that is the one place to reach us. The code is open, so you can also just read what it does.
cs_lede: Every Flobro release is built automatically from the public source code. This page describes who can change that code and how releases get signed.
cs_sign_p: macOS builds are signed and notarized with an Apple Developer ID, so they open without a Gatekeeper warning. Windows builds are not code signed yet, so Windows may show a SmartScreen warning on first run (choose More info, then Run anyway); free signing through SignPath Foundation is planned once the project qualifies. Binaries are produced by GitHub Actions directly from the source repository; no release is built on a personal machine.
cs_roles_li1: Committers and reviewers: members of the flobro organization
cs_roles_p: Changes proposed by anyone outside the team are only merged after review by a team member. Every signing request is approved manually by an approver.
cs_privacy_p: Flobro only transfers the anonymous usage statistics described in the privacy policy, and only when that setting is on. It can be switched off in the app and in the extension.
meta_title: Flobro - Floating browser window, always on top
meta_desc: Flobro is a tiny floating browser window for Mac and Windows. Watch videos, follow dashboards and monitor pages on top of everything, with zero interface until you hover.
og_desc: A tiny floating browser window for Mac and Windows. Zero interface until you hover.
pp_meta_desc: What Flobro collects, what it never collects, and how to switch it off. Privacy explained in plain words, no legalese.
cs_meta_desc: How Flobro builds and signs its releases: who may change the code, who reviews it, and who approves signing.
faq_q1: How do I keep a browser window always on top?
faq_a1: Open Flobro, paste a URL and hit Float. The window stays above every application on Mac and Windows until you unpin it. From Chrome, the Flobro extension floats the current tab and can forward it to the app with one click.
faq_q2: What is the difference between the Chrome extension and the desktop app?
faq_a2: The extension floats a tab in a minimal popup without any browser interface, but browsers cannot keep a window above other applications. The desktop app can, so the extension forwards pages to it for true always on top.
faq_a3: Yes. Flobro is free and open source under the MIT license, without accounts or ads. Development runs on voluntary donations.
faq_q4: Does Flobro track what I browse?
faq_a4: No. Anonymous usage statistics are limited to hostnames only, never full URLs, and can be switched off entirely. The details are in the privacy policy.
hero_ext: Also get the companion Chrome extension
uc_sub: Anything with a URL can float. These are the things Flobro users keep on screen the most.
uc1_p: Keep your CI pipeline, Grafana board or status page in the corner of your screen. Green means keep working; red is impossible to miss.
uc2_p: Float WhatsApp Web, Slack or a live chat next to your work, without giving it a whole monitor.
uc3_p: A match, a talk, a video: snap the window to 16:9, pin it on top and get on with your day.
